title: Gambling Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate gambling operations in a casino. May formulate house rules.

tasks.text: Resolve customer complaints regarding problems, such as payout errors.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Remove suspected cheaters, such as card counters or other players who may have systems that shift the odds of winning to their favor.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Track supplies of money to tables and perform any required paperwork.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Explain and interpret house rules, such as game rules or betting limits.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare work schedules and station arrangements and keep attendance records.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Monitor staffing levels to ensure that games and tables are adequately staffed for each shift, arranging for staff rotations and breaks and locating substitute employees as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Maintain familiarity with all games used at a facility, as well as strategies or tricks employed in those games.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Train new workers or evaluate their performance.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Market or promote the casino to bring in business.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Direct the distribution of complimentary hotel rooms, meals, or other discounts or free items given to players, based on their length of play and betting totals.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Establish policies on issues, such as the type of gambling offered and the odds, the extension of credit, or the serving of food and beverages.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Circulate among gaming tables to ensure that operations are conducted properly, that dealers follow house rules, or that players are not cheating.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set and maintain a bank and table limit for each game.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Direct the compilation of summary sheets that show wager amounts and payoffs for races or events.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Review operational expenses, budget estimates, betting accounts, or collection reports for accuracy.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Record, collect, or pay off bets, issuing receipts as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Notify board attendants of table vacancies so that waiting patrons can play.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.
